title: sorbus hybrida var. gibbsii
intro: Sorbus hybrida var. gibbsii, also known as the American Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its white flowers and bright red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have medicinal benefits in treating various health conditions. The berries of the American Mountain Ash are particularly valued for their high content of vitamin C and anthocyanins, which have been linked to several health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Sorbus hybrida var. gibbsii berries are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are also used in jams and preserves.
